commands.review_summary:
The branch adds tool-name arguments to `Scripts/install_lint_tools.sh` and updates CI/lint wrappers so Linux lint installs SwiftLint only while default installer behavior still installs both lint tools.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a CI/tooling cleanup PR rather than a bug report. Source inspection of current main shows the all-tool install path, and the PR body provides command-level after-fix proof for selective behavior.

target.title: ci: install lint tools selectively
target.body:
## Summary
- teach `Scripts/install_lint_tools.sh` to install only requested pinned lint tools
- make Linux lint preinstall only SwiftLint, since `lint-linux` does not run SwiftFormat
- keep default installer behavior compatible by installing both tools when no tool is specified

## Why
After moving lint to Ubuntu, the Linux lint lane still downloaded SwiftFormat even though it only runs portable checks and SwiftLint. This keeps the same validation coverage while removing an unnecessary download and one extra external dependency from the fast lint path.

## Validation
- workflow YAML parsed with `yaml.safe_load`
- `bash -n Scripts/lint.sh`
- `bash -n Scripts/install_lint_tools.sh`
- `./Scripts/install_lint_tools.sh swiftlint` downloaded only SwiftLint 0.63.2
- `./Scripts/install_lint_tools.sh swiftformat` downloaded only SwiftFormat 0.59.1
- `./Scripts/install_lint_tools.sh` exited with requested tools already installed
- `./Scripts/lint.sh lint-linux` passed: portable checks + SwiftLint across 1153 files, 0 violations
- `./Scripts/lint.sh lint-macos` passed: app locale checks + SwiftFormat, 0 files require formatting

## Exact-head proof

- exact head: `07feb1cd66b82c88da9bd59cde8629613104e40e`
- shell syntax and `make check`: green
- isolated branch autoreview: clean (0.86)
target.author: yuxin-qiao
